name: Mary's Seafood Gumbo
recipeInstructions:
1. Use a large aluminum pot; do not use an iron pot.
2. Wash, stem and cut the okra into half-inch pieces.
3. Season the okra with salt and pepper.
4. Sauté the okra in as little oil as possible until it is nearly done.
5. Add the onions and continue cooking until the okra is tender.
6. Stir in the tomatoes and allow them to simmer.
7. Add the remaining ingredients, including crabmeat, shrimp, crabs, Tabasco sauce, garlic, bay leaf, and thyme.
8. Simmer until everything is cooked through.
Note: If there is a favorite vegetable of the South, it is surely okra. Gumbo gets its name from the Choctaw Indian word 'Kombo' for any thick soup made with okra.
